ayahs.translation: Ta. Sin, Mim.
THESE ARE MESSAGES of the divine writ, clear in itself and clearly showing the truth!

ayahs.arabic: لَعَلَّكَ بَٰخِعٌۭ نَّفْسَكَ أَلَّا يَكُونُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Wouldst thou, perhaps, torment thyself to death [with grief] because they [who live around thee] refuse to believe [in it]?

ayahs.arabic: إِن نَّشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ءَايَةًۭ فَظَلَّتْ أَعْنَٰقُهُمْ لَهَا خَٰضِعِينَ
ayahs.translation: Had We so willed, We could have sent down unto them a message from the skies, so that their necks would [be forced to] bow down before it in humility.

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن ذِكْرٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ مُحْدَثٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهُ مُعْرِضِينَ
ayahs.translation: [But We have not willed it:] and so, whenever there comes unto them any fresh reminder from the Most Gracious, they [who are blind of heart] always - turn their backs upon it:

ayahs.arabic: فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ فَسَيَأْتِيهِمْ أَنۢبَٰٓؤُا۟ مَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ
ayahs.translation: thus, indeed, have they given the lie (to this message as well). But [in time] they will come to understand what it was that they were wont to deride!

ayahs.arabic: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَمْ أَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍۢ كَرِيمٍ
ayahs.translation: Have they, then, never considered the earth - how much of every noble kind [of life] We have caused to grow thereon?

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this, behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: وَإِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبُّكَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱئْتِ ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: HENCE, [remember how it was] when thy Sustainer summoned Moses: “Go unto those evil doing people.
the people of Pharaoh, who refuse to be con­scious of Me!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ أَن يُكَذِّبُونِ
ayahs.translation: He answered: “O my Sustainer! Behold, I fear that they will give me the lie,

ayahs.arabic: وَيَضِيقُ صَدْرِى وَلَا يَنطَلِقُ لِسَانِى فَأَرْسِلْ إِلَىٰ هَٰرُونَ
ayahs.translation: and then my breast will be straitened and my tongue will not be free: send, then, [this Thy command] to Aaron.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَهُمْ عَلَىَّ ذَنۢبٌۭ فَأَخَافُ أَن يَقْتُلُونِ
ayahs.translation: Moreover, they keep a grave charge [pending] against me, and I fear that they will slay me.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ كَلَّا ۖ فَٱذْهَبَا بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّا مَعَكُم مُّسْتَمِعُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said He: “Not so, indeed! Go forth, then, both of you, with Our messages: verify, We shall be with you, listening [to your call]!

ayahs.arabic: فَأْتِيَا فِرْعَوْنَ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: And go, both of you, unto Pharaoh and say, ‘Behold, we bear a message from the Sustainer of all the worlds:
Let the children of Israel go with us!’”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ أَلَمْ نُرَبِّكَ فِينَا وَلِيدًۭا وَلَبِثْتَ فِينَا مِنْ عُمُرِكَ سِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: [But when Moses had delivered his message, Pharaoh] said: “Did we not bring thee up among us when thou wert a child? And didst thou not spend among us years of thy [later] life?

ayahs.arabic: وَفَعَلْتَ فَعْلَتَكَ ٱلَّتِى فَعَلْتَ وَأَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: And yet thou didst commit that [heinous] deed of thine, and [hast thus shown that] thou art one of the ingrate!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ فَعَلْتُهَآ إِذًۭا وَأَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.translation: Replied [Moses]: “I committed it while I was still going astray;

ayahs.arabic: فَفَرَرْتُ مِنكُمْ لَمَّا خِفْتُكُمْ فَوَهَبَ لِى رَبِّى حُكْمًۭا وَجَعَلَنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: and I fled from you because I feared you. But [since] then my Sustainer has endowed me with the ability to judge [between right and wrong], and has made me one of [His] message-­bearers.

ayahs.arabic: وَتِلْكَ نِعْمَةٌۭ تَمُنُّهَا عَلَىَّ أَنْ عَبَّدتَّ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.translation: And [as for] that favour of which thou so tauntingly remindest me - [was it not] due to thy having enslaved the children of Israel?”
Said Pharaoh: “And what [and who] is that ‘Sustainer of all the worlds’?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: [Moses] answered: “[He is] the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them: if you would but [allow yourselves to] be convinced!”
Said [Pharaoh] unto those around him: “Did you hear [what he said]?”
[And Moses] continued: “[He is] your Sus­tainer, [too,] as well as the Sustainer of your fore­fathers of old!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمَجْنُونٌۭ
ayahs.translation: [Pharaoh] exclaimed: “Behold, [this] your ‘apostle’ who [claims that he] has been sent unto you is mad indeed!

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ وَٱلْمَغْرِبِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
ayahs.translation: [But Moses] went on: “[He of whom I speak is] the Sustainer of the east and the west and of all that is between the two”- as you would know] if you would but use your reason!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لَئِنِ ٱتَّخَذْتَ إِلَٰهًا غَيْرِى لَأَجْعَلَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْمَسْجُونِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Pharaoh]: “Indeed, if thou choose to worship any deity other than me, I shall most cer­tainly throw thee into prison!
Said he: “Even if I should bring about before thee something that clearly shows the truth?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ فَأْتِ بِهِۦٓ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: [Pharaoh] answered: “Produce it, then, if thou art a man of truth!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ ثُعْبَانٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon [Moses] threw down his staff - and lo! it was a serpent, plainly visible;

ayahs.arabic: وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِىَ بَيْضَآءُ لِلنَّٰظِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and he drew forth his hand - and lo! it appeared [shining] white to the beholders.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لِلْمَلَإِ حَوْلَهُۥٓ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسَٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Said [pharaoh] unto the great ones around him: “Verily, this is indeed a sorcerer of great knowledge

ayahs.arabic: يُرِيدُ أَن يُخْرِجَكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُم بِسِحْرِهِۦ فَمَاذَا تَأْمُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: who wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery. What, then, do you advise?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ أَرْجِهْ وَأَخَاهُ وَٱبْعَثْ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حَٰشِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “Let him and his brother wait a while, and send unto all cities heralds
who shall assemble before thee all sorcerers of great knowl­edge”
And so the sorcerers were assembled at a set time on a certain day,
and the people were asked: Are you all present,

ayahs.arabic: لَعَلَّنَا نَتَّبِعُ ٱلسَّحَرَةَ إِن كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْغَٰلِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: so that we might follow [in the footsteps of] the sorcerers if it is they who prevail?”

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغَٰلِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Now when the sorcerers came, they said unto Pharaoh: “Verily, we ought to have a great reward if it is we who prevail.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ إِذًۭا لَّمِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Answered he: “Yea - and, verily, in that case you shall be among those who are near unto me.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahs.translation: [And] Moses said unto them: “Throw whatever you are going to throw!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَلْقَوْا۟ حِبَالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلْغَٰلِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon they threw their [magic ropes and their staffs, and said: “By Pharaoh’s might, behold, it is we indeed who have prevailed!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ
ayahs.translation: [But] then Moses threw his staff - and lo! it swallowed up all their deceptions.
And down fell the sorcerers, prostrating them­selves in adoration,
[and] exclaimed: “We have come to believe in the Sustainer of all the worlds,
the Sustainer of Moses and Aaron!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحْرَ فَلَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ۚ لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَٰفٍۢ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Pharaoh]: “Have you come to believe in him ere I have given you permission? Verily, he must be your master who has taught you magic! But in time you shall come to know [my revenge]: most certainly shall I cut off your hands and your feet in great numbers, because of [your] perverseness, and shall most certainly crucify you in great numbers, all together!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَا ضَيْرَ ۖ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “No harm [canst thou do to us]: verily, unto our Sustainer do we turn!

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّا نَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لَنَا رَبُّنَا خَطَٰيَٰنَآ أَن كُنَّآ أَوَّلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Behold, we [but] ardently desire that our Sustainer forgive us our faults in return for our having been foremost among the believers!”

ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِىٓ إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ
ayahs.translation: AND [there came a time when] We inspired Moses thus: “Go forth with My servants by night: for, behold, you will he pursued!”
And Pharaoh sent heralds unto all cities,
[bidding them to call out his troops and to proclaim:] “Behold, these [children of Israel] are but a contemptible band;
but they are indeed filled with hatred of us
seeing that we are, verily, a nation united, fully prepared against danger –
and so we have [rightly] driven them out of [their] gardens and springs,
and [deprived them of their erstwhile] station of honour!”
Thus it was: but [in the course of time] We were to bestow all these [things] as a heritage on the children of Israel.
And so [the Egyptians] caught up with them at sunrise;

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا تَرَٰٓءَا ٱلْجَمْعَانِ قَالَ أَصْحَٰبُ مُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّا لَمُدْرَكُونَ
ayahs.translation: and as soon as the two hosts came in sight of one another, the followers of Moses exclaimed: “Behold, we shall certainly be overtaken [and defeated]!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ كَلَّآ ۖ إِنَّ مَعِىَ رَبِّى سَيَهْدِينِ
ayahs.translation: He replied: Nay indeed! My Sustainer is with me, [and] He will guide me!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْبَحْرَ ۖ فَٱنفَلَقَ فَكَانَ كُلُّ فِرْقٍۢ كَٱلطَّوْدِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon We inspired Moses thus: Strike the sea with thy staff!”- whereupon it parted, and each part appeared like a mountain vast.
And We caused the pursuers to draw near unto that place:
and We saved Moses and all who were with him,
and then We caused the others to drown.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story] behold, there is a message [unto all men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
And yet, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
And convey unto them the story of Abraham –

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ
ayahs.translation: [how it was] when he asked his father and his people, “What is it that you worship?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ أَصْنَامًۭا فَنَظَلُّ لَهَا عَٰكِفِينَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: We worship idols, and we remain ever devoted to them.”
Said he: “Do [you really think that] they hear you when you invoke them,
or benefit you or do you harm?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ بَلْ وَجَدْنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا كَذَٰلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: They exclaimed: But we found our fore­fathers doing the same!”
Said [Abraham]: “Have you, then, ever con­sidered what it is that you have been worshipping
you and those ancient forebears of yours?

ayahs.arabic: فَإِنَّهُمْ عَدُوٌّۭ لِّىٓ إِلَّا رَبَّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “Now [as for me, I know that,] verily, these [false deities] are my enemies, [and that none is my helper] save the Sustainer of all the worlds,
who has created me and is the One who guides me,
and is the One who gives me to eat and to drink,
and when I fall ill, is the One who restores me to health,
and who will cause me to die and then will bring me back to life –

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لِى خَطِيٓـَٔتِى يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahs.translation: and who, [hope, will forgive me my faults on Judgment Day!

ayahs.arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِى حُكْمًۭا وَأَلْحِقْنِى بِٱلصَّٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.translation: “O my Sustainer! Endow me with the ability to judge [between right and wrong], and make me one with the righteous,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱجْعَل لِّى لِسَانَ صِدْقٍۢ فِى ٱلْءَاخِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and grant me the power to convey the truth unto those who will come after me,
and place me among those who shall inherit the garden of bliss!

ayahs.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِأَبِىٓ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.translation: “And forgive my father - for, verily, he is among those who have gone astray –
and do not put me to shame on the Day when all shall be raised from the dead:

ayahs.arabic: يَوْمَ لَا يَنفَعُ مَالٌۭ وَلَا بَنُونَ
ayahs.translation: the Day on which neither wealth will be of any use, nor children,

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍۢ سَلِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: [and when] only he [will be happy] who comes before God with a heart free of evil!”
For, [on that Day,] paradise will be brought within sight of the God-conscious,
whereas the blazing fire will be laid open before those who had been lost in grievous error;

ayahs.arabic: وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ
ayahs.translation: and they will be asked: “Where now is all that you were wont to worship

ayahs.arabic: مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ هَلْ يَنصُرُونَكُمْ أَوْ يَنتَصِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: instead of God? Can these [things and beings] be of any help to you or to themselves?”
Thereupon they will be hurled into hell – they, as well as all [others] who had been lost in grievous error,

words.translation: অতঃপর মুখ নিচু করে ফেলা হবে তাদেরকে

ayahs.translation: and the hosts of Iblis - all together.
And there and then, blaming one another, they [who had grievously sinned in life] will exclaim:

ayahs.arabic: تَٱللَّهِ إِن كُنَّا لَفِى ضَلَٰلٍۢ مُّبِينٍ
ayahs.translation: “By God, we were most obviously astray
when we deemed you [false deities] equal to the Sustainer of all the worlds –
yet they who have seduced us [into believing in you] are the truly guilty ones!
And now we have none to intercede for us,
nor any loving friend.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَوْ أَنَّ لَنَا كَرَّةًۭ فَنَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Would that we had a second chance [in life], so that we could be among the believers!”

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In all this, behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
The people of Noah [too] gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ نُوحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Noah said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.
Hence, remain conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!”
They answered: “Shall we place our faith in thee, even though [only] the most abject [of people] follow thee?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ وَمَا عِلْمِى بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said he: “And what knowledge could I have as to what they were doing [before they came to me]?

ayahs.arabic: إِنْ حِسَابُهُمْ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّى ۖ لَوْ تَشْعُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: Their reckoning rests with none but my Sus­tainer: if you could but understand [this]!
Hence, I shall not drive away [any of] those [who profess to be] believers;
I am nothing but a plain warner.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَٰنُوحُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَرْجُومِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said they: “Indeed, if thou desist not, O Noah, thou wilt surely be stoned to death!”
[Whereupon] he prayed: “O my Sustainer! Behold, my people have given me the lie:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱفْتَحْ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَهُمْ فَتْحًۭا وَنَجِّنِى وَمَن مَّعِىَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: hence, lay Thou wide open the truth between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَنجَيْنَٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلْمَشْحُونِ
ayahs.translation: And so We saved him and those [who were] with him in the fully-laden ark,
and then We caused those who stayed behind to drown.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace
[AND the tribe of] Ad gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ هُودٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Hud said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, con­scious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.
“Will you, in your wanton folly, build [idola­trous] altars on every height,
and make for yourselves mighty castles, [hoping] that you might become immortal?
And will you [always], whenever you lay hand [on others], lay hand [on them] cruelly, without any restraint?
“Be, then, conscious of God and pay heed unto me:
and [thus] be conscious of Him who has [so] amply provided you with all [the good] that you might think of
amply provided you with flocks, and children,
and gardens, and springs -:

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: for, verily, I fear lest suffering befall you on an awesome day!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْنَآ أَوَعَظْتَ أَمْ لَمْ تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْوَٰعِظِينَ
ayahs.translation: [But] they answered. “It is all one to us whether thou preachest [something new] or art not of those who [like to] preach.
This [religion of ours] is none other than that to which our forebears clung,
and we are not going to be chastised [for adhering to it]!”

ayahs.arabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَهْلَكْنَٰهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: And so they gave him the lie: and thereupon We destroyed them. In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
[AND the tribe of] Thamud gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-hearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ صَٰلِحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Salih said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.
“Do you think that you will be left secure [forever] in the midst of what you have here and now?
amidst [these] gardens and springs
and fields, and [these] palm-trees with slender spathes? –
and that you will [always be able to] hew dwellings out of the mountains with [the same] great skill?”

words.translation: এবং তোমরা খোদাই করে নির্মান করেছো

ayahs.translation: “Be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me,
and pay no heed to the counsel of those who are given to excesses –

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ
ayahs.translation: those who spread corruption on earth instead of setting things to rights!”
Said they: “Thou art but one of the be­witched!

ayahs.arabic: مَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُنَا فَأْتِ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: Thou art nothing but a mortal like our­selves! Come, then, forward with a token [of thy mission] if thou art a man of truth!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ هَٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةٌۭ لَّهَا شِرْبٌۭ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍۢ مَّعْلُومٍۢ
ayahs.translation: Replied he: “This she-camel shall have a share of water, and you shall have a share of water, on the days appointed [therefor];

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: and do her no harm, lest suffering befall you on an awesome day!”
But they cruelly slaughtered her - and then they had cause to regret it:

words.translation: কিন্তু তার পায়ের রগ কেটে দিয়ে হত্যা করলো

ayahs.arabic: فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: for the suffering [predicted by Salih,] befell them [then and there]. In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone – is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
[AND] the people of Lot gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ لُوطٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Lot said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.
“Must you, of all people, [lustfully] approach men,

ayahs.arabic: وَتَذَرُونَ مَا خَلَقَ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّنْ أَزْوَٰجِكُم ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ عَادُونَ
ayahs.translation: keeping yourselves aloof from all the [lawful] spouses whom your Sustainer has created for you? Nay, but you are people who transgress all bounds of what is right!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَٰلُوطُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُخْرَجِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said they: “Indeed, If thou desist not, O Lot, thou wilt most certainly be expelled [from this town­ship]!”
[Lot] exclaimed: “Behold, I am one of those who utterly abhor your doings!”
[And then he prayed:] “O my Sustainer! Save me and my household from all that they are doing!”
Thereupon We saved him and all his household –
all but an old woman, who was among those that stayed behind;
and then We utterly destroyed the others,

ayahs.arabic: وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًۭا ۖ فَسَآءَ مَطَرُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and rained down upon them a rain [of destruction]: and dire is such rain upon all who let themselves be warned [to no avail]!

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not be­lieve [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
[AND] the dwellers of the wooded dales [of Madyan] gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ شُعَيْبٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Shu’ayb said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.

ayahs.arabic: ۞ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْمُخْسِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: “[Always] give full measure, and be not among those who [unjustly] cause loss [to others];
and [in all your dealings] weigh with a true balance,

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ
ayahs.translation: and do not deprive people of what is rightfully theirs; and do not act wickedly on earth by spreading corruption,
but be conscious of Him who has created you, just as [He created] those countless generations of old!”
Said they: “Thou art but one of the be­witched,

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُنَا وَإِن نَّظُنُّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلْكَٰذِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: for thou art nothing but a mortal like ourselves! And, behold, we think that thou art a consummate liar!

ayahs.arabic: فَأَسْقِطْ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفًۭا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: Cause, then, fragments of the sky to fall down upon us, if thou art a man of truth!”
Answered [Shu’ayb]: “My Sustainer knows fully well what you are doing.”

ayahs.arabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمِ ٱلظُّلَّةِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahs.translation: But they gave him the lie. And thereupon suffering overtook them on a day dark with shadows: and, verily, it was the suffering of an awesome day!

words.translation: অতঃপর তারা তাকে মিথ্যা সাব্যস্ত করলো

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
Now, behold, this [divine writ] has indeed been bestowed from on high by the Sustainer of all the worlds:
trustworthy divine inspiration has alighted with it from on high
upon thy heart, [O Muhammad] so that thou mayest be among those who preach
in the clear Arabic tongue.
And, verily, [the essence of] this [revelation] is indeed found in the ancient books of divine wisdom [as well].

ayahs.arabic: أَوَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُمْ ءَايَةً أَن يَعْلَمَهُۥ عُلَمَٰٓؤُا۟ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.translation: Is it not evidence enough for them that [so many] learned men from among the children of Israel have recognized this [as true]?
But [even] had We bestowed it from on high upon any of the non-Arabs,

ayahs.arabic: فَقَرَأَهُۥ عَلَيْهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: and had he recited it unto them [in his own tongue], they would not have believed in it.
Thus have We caused this [message] to pass [unheeded] through the hearts of those who are lost in sin:

ayahs.arabic: لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ
ayahs.translation: they will not believe in it till they behold the grievous suffering
that will come upon them [on resurrection,] all of a sudden, without their being aware [of its approach];
and then they will exclaim, “Could we have a respite?”
Do they, then, [really] wish that Our chas­tisement be hastened on?
But hast thou ever considered [this]: If We do allow them to enjoy [this life] for some years,
and thereupon that [chastisement] which they were promised befalls them –

ayahs.arabic: مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُمَتَّعُونَ
ayahs.translation: of what avail to them will be all their past enjoyments?

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا لَهَا مُنذِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: And withal, never have We destroyed any community unless it had been warned
and reminded: for, never do We wrong [anyone].
And [this divine writ is such a reminder:] no evil spirits have brought it down:
for, neither does it suit their ends, nor is it in their power [to impart it to man]:
verily, [even] from hearing it are they utterly debarred!

ayahs.arabic: فَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Hence, [O man,] do not invoke any other deity side by side with God, lest thou find thyself among those who are made to suffer [on Judgment Day].
And warn [whomever thou canst reach, beginning with] thy kinsfolk,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱخْفِضْ جَنَاحَكَ لِمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: and spread the wings of thy tenderness over all of the believers who may follow thee;

ayahs.arabic: فَإِنْ عَصَوْكَ فَقُلْ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌۭ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: but if they disobey thee, say, “I am free of responsibility for aught that you may do!” –
and place thy trust in the Almighty, the Dispenser of Grace,
who sees thee when thou standest [alone],
and [sees] thy behaviour among those who prostrate themselves [before Him]:
for, verily, He alone is all-hearing, all-knowing!

ayahs.arabic: هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ عَلَىٰ مَن تَنَزَّلُ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينُ
ayahs.translation: [And] shall I tell you upon whom it is that those evil spirits descend?
They descend upon all sinful self-deceivers
who readily lend ear [to every falsehood], and most of whom lie to others as well.
And as for the poets [they, too, are prone to deceive themselves: and so, only] those who are lost in grievous error would follow them.

ayahs.arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
ayahs.translation: Art thou not aware that they roam confusedly through all the valleys [of words and thoughts],
and that they [so often] say what they do not do [or feel]?

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَذَكَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًۭا وَٱنتَصَرُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ ۗ وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَىَّ مُنقَلَبٍۢ يَنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: [Most of them are of this kind] save those who have attained to faith, and do righteous deeds, and remember God unceasingly, and defend them­selves [only] after having been wronged, and [trust in God’s promise that] those who are bent on wrong­doing will in time come to know how evil a turn their destinies are bound to take!
